The problem you already feel

Your agents ran. Your audit trail did not.

You can demo the loop. Your buyer asks what executed overnight — and whether it is safe to run again. That question deserves a receipt, not a log dump.

Policy at dispatch — one call, one receipt

→ {"verdict": "BLOCK", "receipt_id": "sa-0886", "signed_at": "…"}

Same spine as sourcea-boot — eval the gate before you buy the build.

Execution Proof Infrastructure

6/6 verification complete <5m live replay demo 100% policy at dispatch Full row capability map

Closeout has no receipt

Your client asks "what ran?" and you forward logs. They wanted a signed PASS or BLOCK — something counsel can file.

Policy arrives too late

Governance at session start misses the dangerous action at minute forty. You need a gate on every dispatch.

Automation without accountability

Workflow tools connect steps. They do not prove outcomes, catch tampering, or let you replay yesterday cold.

01

We run it first

Our factory runs these loops nightly — you inherit the spine we dogfood.

02

Policy before the model

Every action passes your rules in the repository before the LLM executes.

03

Receipts your client keeps

Weekly export with what ran, what blocked, and which policy applied.

04

Proof in one sitting

ALLOW, BLOCK, and tamper detection — screen-shared in under five minutes.

How your loop stays controlled

Dispatch · Receipt · Replay

Dispatch

Each proposed action checked against your policy — every time.

Sub-millisecond checks

Receipt

ALLOW or BLOCK in the repository — Monday starts from evidence, not memory.

Synced with your roster

Replay

Re-run last night without chat history. Tamper shows up immediately.

Demo-ready in minutes
